tool.name: Samtools reheader
tool.description: copy SAM/BAM header between datasets or edit read groups
tool.command: samtools reheader #if str($job.job_type) == 'copy_header' '${job.input_header}' #else ## The command handed to -c is built entirely from tool-directory and ## job-directory paths. The user-supplied @RG values live in the ## $rg_edits config file and are read as data by the awk script, so ## they never reach a shell. -c "awk -f '$__tool_directory__/edit_read_groups.awk' '$rg_edits' -" #end if '${input_file}' ${no_pg} > '${output}'

configfiles.configfile: #slurp #if str($job.job_type) == 'edit_read_groups' #import re #for $field in $job.rg_fields #if str($field.value_source.value_from) == 'element_identifier' #set $rg_value = str($input_file.element_identifier) #set $rg_value = re.sub('(?i)[.](bam|sam|cram)$', '', $rg_value) #else #set $rg_value = str($field.value_source.value) #end if ## The SAM specification restricts @RG field values to printable ASCII ## ([ -~]+). Anything else -- most importantly tabs and newlines, which would ## break both the header and this table -- is replaced. #set $rg_value = re.sub('[^ -~]', '_', $rg_value) ${str($field.tag) + "\t" + $rg_value} #end for #end if

param.name: input_file
param.label: Select target dataset
param.help: The dataset whose header will be replaced or edited. Should be a BAM dataset.

param.name: job_type
param.label: What do you want to do?
param.option: Copy the header of another dataset
Edit read group (@RG) fields in place

param.name: input_header
param.label: Select source dataset
param.help: Header from this dataset will be used to replace the header in the target dataset. May be either SAM or BAM dataset.

param.name: tag
param.label: Field to set
param.help: This field will be set on every @RG record of the header. Fields that are not listed here - in particular ID and PU - are left exactly as they are.

param.name: value_from
param.label: Where should the value come from?
param.option: The name of the target dataset

param.name: value
param.label: Value
param.help: The value to set this field to on every @RG record.

validator.message: @RG field values must consist of printable ASCII characters (the SAM specification allows [ -~]+)

param.name: no_pg
param.label: Keep the new header unmodified?
param.help: By default, samtools reheader will add itself to the chain of tools used to manipulate the target dataset, which will cause one or more @PG records to be inserted into the new header. Activate this option if you want the output to carry the source dataset's header exactly, or - when editing read groups - the edited header and nothing else.

tool.help:
**What it does**
Rewrites the header of a BAM dataset using the ``samtools reheader`` command. The reads themselves are never touched, so read counts and per-read ``RG:Z`` tags are always preserved.
Two modes are available.
**Copy the header of another dataset**
Generates a new BAM dataset with the contents of the *target* dataset, but the header of the *source* dataset.
**Edit read group (@RG) fields in place**
Sets the selected fields on *every* ``@RG`` record of the target dataset's header, adding a field to records that do not have it yet and replacing it on records that do. All other fields are left alone.
This is the mode to use after merging per-lane BAM files into one per-sample BAM file. Aligners typically derive the whole read group from the name of their input, so a merged dataset ends up with one ``@RG`` record per lane whose ``SM`` is a per-lane name. Setting ``SM`` here makes every record agree on the sample while ``ID`` and ``PU`` stay distinct per lane, which is what downstream variant callers expect. Note that this is *not* what tools such as *Picard AddOrReplaceReadGroups* do: those assign every read to a single new read group, which discards the per-lane information.
``ID`` and ``PU`` are deliberately not offered here. They identify individual read groups, so setting them to one value across the header would defeat the purpose of this mode - and, in the case of ``ID``, produce an invalid header with duplicate identifiers.
When this tool is run over a collection, choosing *The name of the target dataset* binds the value to each element's identifier, so every element gets its own value. A fixed text value would be applied unchanged to every element of the collection. A trailing ``.bam``, ``.sam`` or ``.cram`` is removed from the name before it is used, since collection elements often carry one.
Listing the same field twice sets it to the value given last. Editing a header that contains no ``@RG`` records at all fails, rather than quietly returning the input unchanged.
